subject: Changes for Custom Query
commit_message:
Changes for Custom Query

Removed all New files and added their functions to existing old files.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1266
Change-Id: I6ccfcd94f9e9bb751e6f3c868b4a11123e40aea1
Signed-off-by: pramod.jamkhedkar <pramod@research.att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-models-master-verify-java/235/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-models-master-verify-java/235

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2

Something isn't right here - actor.so and actor.vfc are already there. So why are there new files?

Patch Set 2:

> Something isn't right here - actor.so and actor.vfc are already
 > there. So why are there new files?

This is because I didn't want to disturb the existing files which use named queries (incase we need to go back to named queries as a back up option). The new files for SO and VFC make use of custom queries. I can easily go back and delete the existing files (of SO and VFC) and replace them with these new ones, but then it will make it difficult to go back to named queries in case we need to. Let me know what to do.

Patch Set 2:

First of all, NONE of the actors should be making AAI calls directly. The rules should automatically make the call to get the custom query so that all those details are available to all the actors. Now any policy can be written to specify an action on any VNF/VFModule for any actor/recipe. That was the ask - to clean up the AAI code.

Patch Set 2:

> First of all, NONE of the actors should be making AAI calls
 > directly. The rules should automatically make the call to get the
 > custom query so that all those details are available to all the
 > actors. Now any policy can be written to specify an action on any
 > VNF/VFModule for any actor/recipe. That was the ask - to clean up


 > the AAI code.

None of the actors in the new code made a call to A&AI. Infact, in the new code, calls to A&AI are made from only a single function -- in ControlLoopEventManager -- that's it. Its a single line of code from where calls are made to A&AI. 
These SO and VFC classes need to change because the format of custom query response is very different now, and so to populate those requests, you have to change that part of the code, there is no way to get around that.

Patch Set 2:

> First of all, NONE of the actors should be making AAI calls
 > directly. The rules should automatically make the call to get the
 > custom query so that all those details are available to all the
 > actors. Now any policy can be written to specify an action on any
 > VNF/VFModule for any actor/recipe. That was the ask - to clean up
 > the AAI code.

It is not possible to adapt to the new custom query by making changes only to the A&AI code. Given that the format of Custom Query Response is now entirely different, in order to populate the SO request and VFC Request, based on the new response, changes to the createRequest function  of SoServiceProvider is inevitable. 
In the new design, the CustomQuery response has all the information necessary for calculating targets in all use cases (VFC, SO, etc.). So in the new design, the calls are made only via the ControlLoopEventManager's getCqResponse() method. The ControlLoopOperation manager calls this method to get the CqResponse, and call the SO and VFC createRequest methods accordingly, by passing the custom query response.
